author: @WardenclyffeResearch
text: So much wrong with this video.... Tesla died in the New Yorker hotel, never tried to marry a pigeon, Westinghouse didn't finance Wardenclyffe, the Wardenclyffe tower did not burn down, it was dynamited in 1917, not 1906. Tesla was not inspired by Hertz (at all), Tesla was working with radio waves long before Hertz.... etc. Then what is said about a SEC is also rather dubious. A SEC does not have a feed-back coil, bus uses the base of the secondary instead. The kit shown is not a SEC.... :( disappointing....

replies.author: @davidsimoneta8513
replies.text:
I share your disappointement about some information given in this video.
Tesla refused to consider as "hertzian waves" the phenomena behind his attempts to transmit wireless energy...
... unfortunately without a full experimental evidence of his assumpions... at least at planetary scale.😉

author: @jeremiahbullfrog9288
text: Why does the secondary winding have to be a single layer?

replies.author: @hanelyp1
replies.text: The secondary has voltage along the length, each turn adding a little voltage.  Multiple layers puts turns with voltage between them closer, more likely to arc.

replies.author: @jeremiahbullfrog9288
replies.text: @hanelyp1 So you could make a multi-layer tesla coil by separating the layers with a high insulating material? I do wonder why this isn't done

author: @pedjamilosavljevic6235
text:
Tesla has about 300 patents to his name , including one for radio transmitting (which Marconi used for his radio and didn't credited Tesla for his patent). After the trial about that patent , which Markoni won (due to investors and governmental influence) , the ruling was reverted after Tesla's death in 1948 , by a supreme court . 
 He didn't want to marry a pigeon , he was just tired of people's malevolence and he saw a purity in that pigeon . There are letters about that. Though , by his time standards , he was eccentric (he developed some kind of OCD and didn't wanted to  shake hands with people , unless in gloves). I can't help thinking , that his eccentricity would be considered as "quiet private person" today , given the state of the world and overall morality and acceptance of very questionable modes of behavior...  
 He was deceived quite a few times by investors and has been broke few times. He was never about profit , just to have it , but because he wanted money to put into his research . Westinghouse didn't pull out from investment , but he was in some financial trouble and Tesla ripped the contract with him (they were friends) , in order to release him of his obligations , which were $2,5 per 1 HP of electric motors installed and generators , which would made Tesla one of the richest men in the world , ever. Later GP Morgan bought out Westinghouse and he became his main investor , until he asked for more money , to deliver free power to the world (not a good practice for capitalist investor). Besides , he was angry at Tesla , because he promised him a working radio communication (GP was a betting man and he wanted to be first to know the result of horse races in England) and Markoni finished it and marketed it first (Tesla was using money intended for that  , to finance his Wandercliff tower research).
 There was his research in Kolorado , where he managed to light a bulbs very far away from the source , by just putting them in contact with ground. He considered the planet as a big capacitor.
 The fire at his lab (source for conspiracy theory) destroyed most of his research documents and many small scale devices (he never really recovered from that event) , so we don't quite know all the research he did and ,  about hour after his death , FBI took all of his papers , before his nephew got to his hotel (some of which are still not returned). This isn't conspiracy theory. 
 Today , very few of his relatives exist , because Croatian Ustashe (Croatian Nazis) slaughtered most of his family and relatives , just prior to his death , in Smiljan (today's Croatia). Croatians blew up his house (where he was born) in latest Yugoslav wars and they rebuild it some years ago and put his face on Croatian Euro . Stranger things had happened .
 Rob bravely tried to summarize his life and  work in a few minutes , yet serious scientists and researchers still researching his work and life (there are missing links). 
 BTW , as Rob have mentioned , Tesla coil is not so much about wireless transferring of energy at some distance (which it can do , to some degree) , but more about resonance , in order to multiply same amount of input energy (at some distance).

author: @hunnybunnysheavymetalmusic6542
text:
The only problem with "all" EM systems is the risk of cancer.

I can not say just how great this risk is, but it is, as a matter of fact, proven to exist, at various levels of AC voltage, and is particularly demonstrable near national high tension lines, here in the USA.

Therefore, it is questionable as to just how safe Tesla's free energy system would have been.

Admittedly, I have full confidence that his design would, did, and still does work, only that it is potentially quite dangerous because of the effects of high energy EM radiation being emitted from the system.

author: @bobweiram6321
text: You should research Tesla's history more carefully. He wasn't what any reasonable person would categorize as a humanitarian. In fact, he was an unabashed eugenicist who believe in the forced sterilization of those considered physically and intellectually "unfit." There are a few quotes where he expressed racist beliefs towards other racial and ethnic groups. Clearly, he may have been a great scientist, but he wasn't a person who cared for all of humanity.

author: @shawncalderon4950
text:
Great presentation. No sparks, though:(
Use a MOSFET with a heat sink, and wind .2 or .3mm wire. 
.6 mm wire is for big Tesla coils. 
Place an electrolytic capacitor across the input voltage. 
Use a potentiometer instead of a resistor. 
This will provide some sparks.

author: @kevinleebailey
text:
Radio transmission is basically wireless power transmission. The closer to the transmitter the more power can be received. 
Take a look at Don L Smith's research.
I'm charging the battery out of a disposable vape using a loop antenna crystal radio tuned to radio five live or talk sport and running two white LEDs as a night light. I'm about 4 miles away from the nearest transmitter.

author: @Kangsteri
text: Slayer exciter is not Tesla coil. The real Hairpin circuit separates the load from the source, it also needs to be tuned. So it doesn't burn, it is adapting to the natural frequency of the load. Slayer exciter will cause damage in the nerves, Tesla coil doesn't. Beware of this with high current!

author: @jedclampett3725
text: Looks like you missed one of the most important patents that were part of the communications system. The coil for electromagnets patent. The exiter is nothing if it doesn't adjust to the coils resonant frequency. He avoided the discharges all the builders go for, his intent was to build up the power and make it resonate to have 'spooky action at a distance' as explained by Maxwell. The discharges destroy the insulation and discharge the coil, which was not only contrary to what he attempted to achieve and quite expensive considering the scales of power levels he was working with. It was also something he did to impress investors and the easily impressed... a show of power with no aim, if you will.

author: @Cryptic_Triptych
text:
Unfortunately, most people don't understand that if the Tesla coil is giving off sparks, you are running it backwards. 

BTW, the Wardenclyffe tower didn't burn down. NT couldn't get his loan extended, from JP Morgan, to finish the project. Additionally, supplying the public with nearly no cost electricity was a non-profitable business case for JP Morgan to finance.

author: @kuhrd
text: Using a much more suitable transitor or mosfet that are heatsinked in this circuit along with some protection diodes and about 4-8X the number of turns along with a low esr capacitor on the input allows the use of much higher input voltage which allows the lighting of FL tubes from several feet or more along with a continuous corona discharge.

author: @scotttovey
text:
The closeness necessary for it to light makes it impractical to use with most lighting scenarios. However, it would be both practical and safer for high voltage battery charging as it would eliminate the need to connect two metal contacts together when plugging the device in for charging.

The modern way of using a flat coil for wireless charging is perhaps bit more practical.
